revision.text:
[[ja:伊賀留我神社 (四日市市羽津)]] {{Expand Japanese|伊賀留我神社 (四日市市羽津)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Igatomega Shrine | native_name = 伊賀留我神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Igatomega Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.008315|136.633359}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Igatomega Shrine'''|伊賀留我神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.008315|136.633359}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039400|ltq=Q135039400|qid=Q135039400|1=Ikarukano Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県四日市市羽津戊', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊賀留我神社 (四日市市羽津)]] {{wikidata link|Q135069245}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Ikaruga Shrine | Image = {{Image needed|cat=Yokkaichi City}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|qid=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yokkaichi City|Q26410|qid=Q26410|1=Yokkaichi}} Hazu Bo 523 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aramitama|1=UNKNOWN}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = | Main hall = | Other name = | Pilgrimage sites, etc. = | Annual festival = October 16 | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Ikaruga
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Ikaruga Shrine''' (いかるがじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in the {{ill|lt=Hazu area|Q109363581|qid=Q109363581|lt=Hazu|1=Hazu}} of {{ill|lt=Yokkaichi City|Q26410|qid=Q26410|1=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|qid=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (a shrine listed in the Engishiki), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called Minami-Ikaruga Shrine{{Cite book|Japanese book |title=History of Yokkaichi City |date=1961-3-31 |publisher=Yokkaichi City Hall |page=1616}}. In Shigefuku, Yokkaichi City, there is Kita {{ill|lt=Ikaruga Shrine (Shigefuku, Yokkaichi City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikaruga Shrine}} (its enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōhirume no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōhirume no Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|qid=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}). === History === The year of foundation is unknown. It is said that in the 18th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, when {{ill|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|qid=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} was traveling in search of a site for the grand shrine of {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, she stopped in this area, and the deity was enshrined at that place{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%bc%8a%e8%b3%80%e7%95%99%e6%88%91%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-11-24}}. During the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era, the village was divided into north and south, becoming Shigefuku Village of the {{ill|lt=Kuwana Domain|Q2887780|qid=Q2887780|1=Kuwana Domain}} and Hazu Village, an enclave of the {{ill|lt=Oshi Domain|Q3034405|qid=Q3034405|1=Oshi Domain}}, and as a result, Ikaruga Shrine also came to exist side by side as northern and southern shrines{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Ikaruga Shrine (Shigefuku Kō)|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e4%bc%8a%e8%b3%80%e7%95%99%e6%88%91%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-2/|access-date=2025-12-13}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deity: Amaterasu Ōmikami Aramitama * (Enshrined together) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}・Ōyamatsumi no Kami・{{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}・one unidentified deity === Annual festival === October 16 === Access === About 2 km north of {{ill|lt=Kasumigaura Station|Q6374902|qid=Q6374902|lt=Kasumigaura Station|1=Kasumigaura Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagoya Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973|qid=Q11378973|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikaruga Shrine (Hatsu, Yokkaichi City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikaruga Shrine (Hatsu, Yokkaichi City)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokkaichi, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yokkaichi, Mie]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Mie Prefecture]]
